methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $182M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Standard Nuclear, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Kurt Terrani, operates within a 11-company comparable universe spanning 3 public players (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private-stage peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, and Kairos Power. The company has raised $182M to date.

Valuation analysis employed stage-progression methodology, the sole applicable triangulation approach given insufficient narrow-peer clustering. Using $182M in total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A progression to the next funding stage yields $364M. This method assumes Standard Nuclear's cost structure and growth trajectory align with sector norms at its stage.

Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to sparse same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables; the broader nuclear energy peer set of 8 companies did not yield the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable median extraction. The single-method triangulation supports a median valuation of $364M, with a range between $309M (low) and $419M (high) reflecting sensitivity around stage-progression assumptions. Key uncertainties include: capital efficiency relative to peers, engineering validation milestones, and regulatory pathway clarity—all material to whether Standard Nuclear warrants premium or discount to the derived multiple.
